steps.canonical_text: If the infant can cough or cry, let them keep coughing and watch closely. Do not put your fingers in their mouth.
must_keep_keywords: Do not put your fingers in their mouth

steps.canonical_text: If they cannot cough, breathe, or make sound, lay the infant face down along your forearm with the head lower than the body, supporting the jaw. Give 5 firm back blows between the shoulder blades with the heel of your hand.
Turn the infant face up, still with the head lower than the body. Place two fingers on the lower half of the breastbone and give 5 chest thrusts about 4 centimeters deep.
Keep alternating 5 back blows and 5 chest thrusts until the object comes out, the infant cries or breathes, or they become unresponsive.
If the infant becomes unresponsive, call emergency services now and start infant CPR: 30 chest compressions with two fingers, then 2 small rescue breaths.
